diagrams.title: Thread State Transition Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the five states of a thread and transitions between them
elements: Arrows showing transitions: NEW→READY, READY→RUNNING, RUNNING→READY, RUNNING→BLOCKED, RUNNING→TERMINATED, BLOCKED→READY
diagrams.purpose: Understanding thread lifecycle and when transitions occur

diagrams.title: Ready Queue Structure
diagrams.description: Shows how threads wait in ready queue for CPU allocation in Round-Robin
elements: Thread T1 (quantum expired) moves to back
Queue maintains FIFO order for equal priority
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how Round-Robin schedules threads fairly

diagrams.title: Multithreading Models Comparison
diagrams.description: Three models showing user threads to kernel thread mapping
elements: Many-to-One: Multiple user threads → 1 kernel thread
Many-to-Many: Multiple user threads → multiple (fewer or equal) kernel threads
diagrams.purpose: Understanding advantages and limitations of each threading model
